title: Memory Management and Memory Safety in Modern C23
description: Learners who can reason about data structures and algorithms but are new to C and need to design, test, diagnose, optimize, and defend explicit memory and resource lifetimes from first principles through production systems. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate c23 foundations needed for memory work; translation, types, conversions, objects, lifetime, representation, alignment, arrays, pointers, strings, structures, unions, callbacks, automatic storage, heap allocation, size arithmetic, ownership, cleanup, realloc, temporal and spatial defects, initialization, undefined behavior, aliasing, provenance, arenas, pools, reference counting, tracing collection, external resources, concurrency, abi, ffi, webassembly linear memory, sanitizers, static analysis, fuzzing, profiling, hardening, testing, release, rollback, and incident defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate more than two hundred exact runnable changed-case c programs in isolated browser practice, plus explicit c23, gcc 16, clang 22, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, abi, thread, python, rust, webassembly, load, fault, release, and production transfer evidence through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative stakeholder projects and a performance-based memory-safety certification defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves earlier size, bounds, lifetime, ownership, cleanup, accessibility, security, testing, and evidence habits before adding one bounded memory concept.
Every coding task produces changed-case output and an inspectable invariant. Code shape alone, a clean compile, one passing browser run, or one sanitizer run is never sufficient mastery evidence.

Browser code stays inside the PicoC subset and disposable worker. C23 syntax and semantics, optimizing compilers, native libraries, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production claims name an explicit controlled transfer gate.
Unsafe examples are presented as defects to predict, diagnose, repair, and regression-test; they are never copied into starter patterns as acceptable production code.

competencies.statement: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.
misconceptions: A browser interpreter proves current native compiler, sanitizer, ABI, and operating-system behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.

competencies.statement: Pin ISO/IEC 9899:2024, reviewed implementation support, diagnostics, extensions, and target ABI as a compatibility contract.
misconceptions: Selecting a C23 mode makes every C23 facility available and identical on every compiler and library.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin ISO/IEC 9899:2024, reviewed implementation support, diagnostics, extensions, and target ABI as a compatibility contract.

competencies.statement: Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.
misconceptions: If a program starts, every earlier translation and linkage assumption was correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.

competencies.statement: Define spatial, temporal, initialization, type, ownership, resource, and concurrency properties as observable claims.
misconceptions: Memory safety means only that a program does not visibly crash.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define spatial, temporal, initialization, type, ownership, resource, and concurrency properties as observable claims.

competencies.statement: State what tests, warnings, analyzers, sanitizers, proofs, reviews, and production telemetry can and cannot establish.
misconceptions: One clean sanitizer run proves absence of memory defects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State what tests, warnings, analyzers, sanitizers, proofs, reviews, and production telemetry can and cannot establish.

competencies.statement: Predict macro expansion, conditional compilation, include guards, and header ownership before translation.
misconceptions: The preprocessor follows C scope and type rules.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict macro expansion, conditional compilation, include guards, and header ownership before translation.

competencies.statement: Distinguish declarations, tentative and full definitions, linkage, compatible types, and one-definition responsibilities.
misconceptions: Repeating a declaration and repeating a definition have the same program effect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish declarations, tentative and full definitions, linkage, compatible types, and one-definition responsibilities.

competencies.statement: Use complete prototypes and compatible parameter and return types across every caller boundary.
misconceptions: A linker can validate argument count, promotions, and return types between separately translated files.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use complete prototypes and compatible parameter and return types across every caller boundary.

competencies.statement: Compile with strict language mode, high-value warnings, warnings as errors, debug information, and a documented extension policy.
misconceptions: No warning means the source has defined behavior and a correct interface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compile with strict language mode, high-value warnings, warnings as errors, debug information, and a documented extension policy.

competencies.statement: Diagnose duplicate, missing, hidden, weak, and mismatched symbols from object and linker evidence.
misconceptions: Every undefined symbol is caused by forgetting to write the function body.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose duplicate, missing, hidden, weak, and mismatched symbols from object and linker evidence.

competencies.statement: Select integer types from range, signedness, rank, width, and representation requirements rather than familiar spelling.
misconceptions: int, long, and pointers have fixed widths on all supported targets.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select integer types from range, signedness, rank, width, and representation requirements rather than familiar spelling.

competencies.statement: Trace integer promotions and usual arithmetic conversions before comparing, shifting, indexing, or sizing.
misconceptions: A negative signed value stays negative when compared with an unsigned size.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace integer promotions and usual arithmetic conversions before comparing, shifting, indexing, or sizing.

competencies.statement: Use size_t for object sizes, ptrdiff_t for valid pointer differences, and guarded conversions at API boundaries.
misconceptions: size_t is always unsigned long and therefore safe for arbitrary subtraction.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use size_t for object sizes, ptrdiff_t for valid pointer differences, and guarded conversions at API boundaries.

competencies.statement: Reject overflowing addition, multiplication, alignment, and growth calculations before allocation using portable checked patterns or C23 checked arithmetic.
misconceptions: malloc reports an error when its size expression overflowed before the call.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject overflowing addition, multiplication, alignment, and growth calculations before allocation using portable checked patterns or C23 checked arithmetic.

competencies.statement: Evaluate C23 _BitInt and implementation limits without assuming a novel width improves ABI portability.
misconceptions: An exact bit width automatically has the same calling convention and library support everywhere.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate C23 _BitInt and implementation limits without assuming a novel width improves ABI portability.

competencies.statement: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.
misconceptions: A pointer value is the object itself.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.

competencies.statement: Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.
misconceptions: File scope implies static linkage and immutable lifetime.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.

competencies.statement: Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.
misconceptions: Allocated storage has a live typed object from malloc until free regardless of how it is accessed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.

competencies.statement: Treat a reused address as a new lifetime and preserve generation or identity evidence where stale handles are possible.
misconceptions: The same numeric address proves the same object still exists.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat a reused address as a new lifetime and preserve generation or identity evidence where stale handles are possible.

competencies.statement: Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.
misconceptions: const owns a value and volatile makes concurrent access race-free.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.

competencies.statement: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.
misconceptions: Every byte pattern is a valid value for every C type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.

competencies.statement: Compute and satisfy fundamental, extended, member, and allocated alignment requirements before access.
misconceptions: malloc alignment and packed structures make every cast suitably aligned.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute and satisfy fundamental, extended, member, and allocated alignment requirements before access.

competencies.statement: Measure rather than assume member offsets, tail padding, and layout changes across ABI targets.
misconceptions: Structure members are always adjacent in declaration order with no unused bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure rather than assume member offsets, tail padding, and layout changes across ABI targets.

competencies.statement: Encode and decode external integers byte-by-byte or with defined conversion APIs instead of dumping native objects.
misconceptions: Endianness is the only portability problem in raw structure serialization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Encode and decode external integers byte-by-byte or with defined conversion APIs instead of dumping native objects.

competencies.statement: Use C23 memset_explicit or a reviewed non-elidable alternative for secrets and verify compiler and library support.
misconceptions: Ordinary memset is guaranteed to remain when the cleared object is no longer read.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use C23 memset_explicit or a reviewed non-elidable alternative for secrets and verify compiler and library support.

competencies.statement: Preserve array extent where possible and predict array-to-pointer conversion at expressions and parameters.
misconceptions: An array parameter carries its declared bound into the called function.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve array extent where possible and predict array-to-pointer conversion at expressions and parameters.

competencies.statement: Perform pointer arithmetic only within one array object or one-past it and avoid dereferencing one-past pointers.
misconceptions: Numerically adjacent allocations form one legal pointer-arithmetic range.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Perform pointer arithmetic only within one array object or one-past it and avoid dereferencing one-past pointers.

competencies.statement: State and check lower and upper index invariants before access, including zero-length and changed-shape cases.
misconceptions: Checking only index < length is sufficient when index may be signed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State and check lower and upper index invariants before access, including zero-length and changed-shape cases.

competencies.statement: Represent a borrowed slice with pointer, length, mutability, lifetime, and aliasing contracts that travel together.
misconceptions: A non-null pointer reveals how many elements may be read or written.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent a borrowed slice with pointer, length, mutability, lifetime, and aliasing contracts that travel together.

competencies.statement: Calculate multidimensional offsets with checked dimensions, row stride, element size, and layout evidence.
misconceptions: A pointer-to-pointer is interchangeable with a contiguous two-dimensional array.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Calculate multidimensional offsets with checked dimensions, row stride, element size, and layout evidence.

competencies.statement: Distinguish C strings, unterminated character arrays, byte buffers, string literals, and encoded text.
misconceptions: Every char pointer names a writable null-terminated string.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish C strings, unterminated character arrays, byte buffers, string literals, and encoded text.

competencies.statement: Track logical length, allocated capacity, required terminator space, and maximum scan distance separately.
misconceptions: A capacity of n always permits n visible characters plus a terminator.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Track logical length, allocated capacity, required terminator space, and maximum scan distance separately.

competencies.statement: Use size-aware copying and formatting with return-value checks and truncation policy.
misconceptions: Replacing strcpy with strncpy automatically produces a valid terminated string.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use size-aware copying and formatting with return-value checks and truncation policy.

competencies.statement: Validate and preserve encoding at system boundaries without splitting multibyte sequences or equating bytes with characters.
misconceptions: char stores one user-visible character.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate and preserve encoding at system boundaries without splitting multibyte sequences or equating bytes with characters.

competencies.statement: Recognize unbounded and ambiguous string APIs as defect-analysis material and migrate them behind tested contracts.
misconceptions: A larger destination makes an unbounded legacy copy safe enough.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recognize unbounded and ambiguous string APIs as defect-analysis material and migrate them behind tested contracts.

competencies.statement: Initialize, copy, compare, and validate structures by members and invariants rather than raw-byte assumptions.
misconceptions: memcmp is a portable semantic equality test for structures.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Initialize, copy, compare, and validate structures by members and invariants rather than raw-byte assumptions.

competencies.statement: Pair a union with an explicit discriminant and access only the active, validated member.
misconceptions: Reading a different union member is a portable conversion mechanism for all representations.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pair a union with an explicit discriminant and access only the active, validated member.

competencies.statement: Allocate flexible-array objects with checked header-plus-elements arithmetic and record their element count.
misconceptions: sizeof a flexible-array structure includes space for the requested payload.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allocate flexible-array objects with checked header-plus-elements arithmetic and record their element count.

competencies.statement: Confine bit-fields to implementation-characterized layouts and use masks for external formats.
misconceptions: Bit-field order and packing follow declaration order on every ABI.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Confine bit-fields to implementation-characterized layouts and use masks for external formats.

competencies.statement: Hide representation behind incomplete types and constructor, observer, mutation, and destructor contracts.
misconceptions: Publishing structure fields cannot constrain future ABI-compatible changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Hide representation behind incomplete types and constructor, observer, mutation, and destructor contracts.

competencies.statement: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
misconceptions: Any function address can be cast and called through any callback type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.

competencies.statement: Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
misconceptions: Registering a callback copies or extends the life of its context automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.

competencies.statement: Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
misconceptions: A library may allocate with one heap and let any caller free with another.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.

competencies.statement: Leave out-parameters in documented states on success and every failure, with ownership transfer made explicit.
misconceptions: A function that fails before its final line cannot have changed an out-parameter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Leave out-parameters in documented states on success and every failure, with ownership transfer made explicit.

competencies.statement: Document nullability, bounds, aliasing, mutation, ownership, lifetime, errors, and thread rules at declarations.
misconceptions: A descriptive function name is a sufficient memory contract.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document nullability, bounds, aliasing, mutation, ownership, lifetime, errors, and thread rules at declarations.

competencies.statement: Keep pointers to automatic objects within the execution and subobject lifetimes that make them valid.
misconceptions: A returned pointer remains valid if the stack bytes have not yet been overwritten.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep pointers to automatic objects within the execution and subobject lifetimes that make them valid.

competencies.statement: Bound recursion by adversarial input depth, frame cost, and target stack limits or replace it with explicit storage.
misconceptions: Tail recursion is guaranteed to consume constant stack space in C.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound recursion by adversarial input depth, frame cost, and target stack limits or replace it with explicit storage.

competencies.statement: Use variable-length arrays only under explicit positive bounds and stack budgets, with portable alternatives.
misconceptions: A VLA allocation fails like malloc and can be checked for null.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use variable-length arrays only under explicit positive bounds and stack budgets, with portable alternatives.

competencies.statement: Treat alloca-like facilities as nonstandard stack allocation with lifetime, overflow, loop, and portability hazards.
misconceptions: alloca is a faster malloc with equivalent failure and release behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat alloca-like facilities as nonstandard stack allocation with lifetime, overflow, loop, and portability hazards.

competencies.statement: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.
misconceptions: Only return statements can cause a local address to escape.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.

competencies.statement: Apply malloc and calloc size, initialization, alignment, zero-size, and failure contracts without dereferencing assumptions.
misconceptions: calloc is merely malloc followed by a universally equivalent zero value for every type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply malloc and calloc size, initialization, alignment, zero-size, and failure contracts without dereferencing assumptions.

competencies.statement: Propagate allocation failure without partial-state leaks, null dereferences, or impossible-error assumptions.
misconceptions: Small allocations cannot fail on a modern virtual-memory system.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate allocation failure without partial-state leaks, null dereferences, or impossible-error assumptions.

competencies.statement: Choose and test a project policy for zero elements rather than depending on an implementation-specific returned pointer.
misconceptions: malloc(0) must return null and may therefore be used as a portable emptiness test.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose and test a project policy for zero elements rather than depending on an implementation-specific returned pointer.

competencies.statement: Satisfy size-multiple, alignment, release-family, and implementation-support rules for aligned storage.
misconceptions: Any power-of-two alignment request is portable and free always matches every aligned allocator.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Satisfy size-multiple, alignment, release-family, and implementation-support rules for aligned storage.

competencies.statement: Adopt C23 free_sized and free_aligned_sized only behind exact size, alignment, availability, and fallback contracts.
misconceptions: Supplying an approximate size to a sized deallocation is an optimization-only mistake.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Adopt C23 free_sized and free_aligned_sized only behind exact size, alignment, availability, and fallback contracts.

competencies.statement: Prove count times element-size representability before calling allocation functions.
misconceptions: A cast to size_t before multiplication prevents wraparound.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove count times element-size representability before calling allocation functions.

competencies.statement: Check header, padding, alignment, terminator, and payload additions in a safe order.
misconceptions: Only multiplication can overflow allocation formulas.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Check header, padding, alignment, terminator, and payload additions in a safe order.

competencies.statement: Characterize whether the target calloc detects product overflow while retaining caller-side policy and tests.
misconceptions: Every conforming calloc must diagnose multiplication overflow identically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Characterize whether the target calloc detects product overflow while retaining caller-side policy and tests.

competencies.statement: Use POSIX reallocarray where available with feature tests and a portable transactional fallback.
misconceptions: reallocarray is part of every C23 standard library.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use POSIX reallocarray where available with feature tests and a portable transactional fallback.

competencies.statement: Derive maximum capacity from object limit, element size, metadata, and stakeholder workload before allocation.
misconceptions: If the arithmetic fits size_t, the requested object is operationally acceptable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive maximum capacity from object limit, element size, metadata, and stakeholder workload before allocation.

competencies.statement: Classify each pointer as unique owner, shared owner, mutable borrow, immutable borrow, weak observation, or non-owning handle.
misconceptions: Pointer syntax reveals ownership automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify each pointer as unique owner, shared owner, mutable borrow, immutable borrow, weak observation, or non-owning handle.

competencies.statement: Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.
misconceptions: Passing a pointer to a function always transfers responsibility to free it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.

competencies.statement: Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.
misconceptions: const extends a borrowed object lifetime.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.

competencies.statement: Prevent surprising mutation through aliases with interface design, copying, or explicit synchronization.
misconceptions: Two restrict-qualified pointers are checked for non-aliasing at runtime.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent surprising mutation through aliases with interface design, copying, or explicit synchronization.

competencies.statement: Review public APIs with an ownership table covering inputs, outputs, globals, nested allocations, and cleanup.
misconceptions: A destructor alone makes a multi-allocation API ownership-complete.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Review public APIs with an ownership table covering inputs, outputs, globals, nested allocations, and cleanup.

competencies.statement: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.
misconceptions: A partially initialized structure can always use the normal destructor unchanged.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.

competencies.statement: Release acquired resources in dependency-safe reverse order with idempotent or guarded cleanup.
misconceptions: Cleanup order does not matter after an error.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Release acquired resources in dependency-safe reverse order with idempotent or guarded cleanup.

competencies.statement: Use structured goto cleanup when it centralizes unwinding without skipping initialization or obscuring ownership.
misconceptions: Every goto in C is evidence of poor control flow.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use structured goto cleanup when it centralizes unwinding without skipping initialization or obscuring ownership.

competencies.statement: Build replacement state separately and commit only after validation so callers retain a usable old value on failure.
misconceptions: Returning an error automatically rolls back earlier writes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build replacement state separately and commit only after validation so callers retain a usable old value on failure.

competencies.statement: Preserve the first causal error while still recording cleanup failures and resource state.
misconceptions: The last cleanup error is always the most useful return value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve the first causal error while still recording cleanup failures and resource state.

competencies.statement: Store realloc results transactionally so allocation failure preserves the original live block.
misconceptions: Assigning realloc directly to the owner is safe because failure frees the old block.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Store realloc results transactionally so allocation failure preserves the original live block.

competencies.statement: Invalidate or rebase all interior pointers and aliases when realloc may move storage.
misconceptions: Only the owner pointer changes when realloc moves a block.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Invalidate or rebase all interior pointers and aliases when realloc may move storage.

competencies.statement: Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.
misconceptions: Doubling capacity is universally optimal and cannot overflow.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.

competencies.statement: Define shrink success, failure, fragmentation, hysteresis, and old-value preservation rather than assuming space returns.
misconceptions: A smaller realloc always moves less memory and immediately reduces process RSS.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define shrink success, failure, fragmentation, hysteresis, and old-value preservation rather than assuming space returns.

competencies.statement: Avoid ambiguous zero-size realloc dependencies by handling logical empty state explicitly.
misconceptions: realloc(pointer, 0) has one portable free-and-null outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Avoid ambiguous zero-size realloc dependencies by handling logical empty state explicitly.

competencies.statement: Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
misconceptions: The crash site is necessarily where the object was freed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.

competencies.statement: Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
misconceptions: Setting one local pointer to null prevents other aliases from freeing the block.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.

competencies.statement: Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
misconceptions: An interior pointer remains usable for its offset after the base is freed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.

competencies.statement: Use nulling as a local state aid without claiming it repairs aliases, temporal races, or ownership ambiguity.
misconceptions: Free automatically changes every copy of the pointer to null.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use nulling as a local state aid without claiming it repairs aliases, temporal races, or ownership ambiguity.

competencies.statement: Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.
misconceptions: Address uniqueness across time is a dependable object identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.

competencies.statement: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.
misconceptions: Testing length one and a typical length catches every off-by-one defect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.

competencies.statement: Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.
misconceptions: The destination size alone determines whether a copy is safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.

competencies.statement: Choose memcpy only for proven non-overlap and memmove for overlapping byte ranges.
misconceptions: memcpy implementations always behave like memmove on small overlaps.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose memcpy only for proven non-overlap and memmove for overlapping byte ranges.

competencies.statement: Prevent subtraction and backward scans from forming pointers before the first element.
misconceptions: A pointer may temporarily move before an array if it is not dereferenced.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent subtraction and backward scans from forming pointers before the first element.

competencies.statement: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.
misconceptions: A fortified build makes all dynamic buffer operations memory safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.

competencies.statement: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
misconceptions: malloc and automatic storage begin as zero-filled objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.

competencies.statement: Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.
misconceptions: Reading any uninitialized unsigned integer merely gives an unpredictable ordinary value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.

competencies.statement: Explain how undefined behavior removes semantic constraints and invalidates debug-build intuition.
misconceptions: Undefined behavior must produce a crash or diagnostic at the offending statement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain how undefined behavior removes semantic constraints and invalidates debug-build intuition.

competencies.statement: Distinguish undefined, unspecified, implementation-defined, locale-specific, and defined behavior and collect required documentation.
misconceptions: All portable variability is undefined behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish undefined, unspecified, implementation-defined, locale-specific, and defined behavior and collect required documentation.

competencies.statement: Use empty initialization and reviewed C23 facilities where supported while preserving explicit invariants and fallback tests.
misconceptions: New syntax removes the need to reason about subobject initialization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use empty initialization and reviewed C23 facilities where supported while preserving explicit invariants and fallback tests.

competencies.statement: Access allocated and copied storage through types permitted by effective-type rules or defined character-byte paths.
misconceptions: Any suitably aligned pointer cast creates an object of the destination type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Access allocated and copied storage through types permitted by effective-type rules or defined character-byte paths.

competencies.statement: Preserve optimizer-visible non-aliasing rules and test any deliberate compiler-extension policy.
misconceptions: Turning off one optimization flag gives portable semantics to invalid aliasing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve optimizer-visible non-aliasing rules and test any deliberate compiler-extension policy.

competencies.statement: Use memcpy into a live destination object for representation transfer and validate the resulting value domain.
misconceptions: A byte copy makes every source representation a valid destination value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use memcpy into a live destination object for representation transfer and validate the resulting value domain.

competencies.statement: Treat pointer-integer conversions as implementation-defined boundaries and avoid manufacturing dereference authority from numbers.
misconceptions: Equal uintptr_t values always carry equivalent dereference provenance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat pointer-integer conversions as implementation-defined boundaries and avoid manufacturing dereference authority from numbers.

competencies.statement: Apply ISO/IEC TS 6010 provenance concepts as reviewed guidance without mislabeling them as universally implemented C23 behavior.
misconceptions: The provenance technical specification is already identical to every production compiler memory model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply ISO/IEC TS 6010 provenance concepts as reviewed guidance without mislabeling them as universally implemented C23 behavior.

competencies.statement: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
misconceptions: A bump allocator cannot overflow because it only adds small objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.

competencies.statement: Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
misconceptions: Arena reset is equivalent to individually running every object destructor.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.

competencies.statement: Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.
misconceptions: A singly linked free list needs no corruption checks because nodes are fixed size.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.

competencies.statement: Choose size classes, alignment, page ownership, and internal-fragmentation metrics from workload evidence.
misconceptions: One size class minimizes both waste and allocator overhead.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose size classes, alignment, page ownership, and internal-fragmentation metrics from workload evidence.

competencies.statement: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.
misconceptions: RSS alone identifies allocator fragmentation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.

competencies.statement: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
misconceptions: Every pointer copy should increment a reference count.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.

competencies.statement: Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.
misconceptions: Reference counts cannot overflow in realistic programs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.

competencies.statement: Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.
misconceptions: A reference count reaching zero eventually is guaranteed if the program stops using an object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.

competencies.statement: Validate weak handles against liveness and generation before acquiring a new strong reference.
misconceptions: A weak pointer can be safely inspected after the object is destroyed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate weak handles against liveness and generation before acquiring a new strong reference.

competencies.statement: Evaluate atomicity, contention, batching, deferred release, and memory-order requirements for shared counts.
misconceptions: Making the count atomic makes the whole referenced object thread-safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate atomicity, contention, batching, deferred release, and memory-order requirements for shared counts.

competencies.statement: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.
misconceptions: An unreachable object is necessarily unused and safe to finalize immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.

competencies.statement: Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.
misconceptions: A single graph walk is enough without a complete allocation registry.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.

competencies.statement: Explain false retention and invalid reclamation risks when arbitrary words may be treated as pointers.
misconceptions: Conservative collection finds every true pointer and no false pointer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain false retention and invalid reclamation risks when arbitrary words may be treated as pointers.

competencies.statement: Constrain finalizer order, reentrancy, resurrection, external resources, and repeated collection behavior.
misconceptions: A garbage collector provides deterministic file and lock release.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain finalizer order, reentrancy, resurrection, external resources, and repeated collection behavior.

competencies.statement: Relate generational and concurrent collection to write barriers, mutator cooperation, pause, throughput, and memory tradeoffs.
misconceptions: Concurrent collection removes every stop-the-world phase and synchronization cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate generational and concurrent collection to write barriers, mutator cooperation, pause, throughput, and memory tradeoffs.

competencies.statement: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.
misconceptions: Memory leak tools detect every leaked file, descriptor, socket, and lock.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model external handles as scarce lifetime-bound resources even when their storage is not heap memory.

competencies.statement: Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.
misconceptions: Closing or destroying a resource twice is harmless if free(NULL) is harmless.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.

competencies.statement: Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.
misconceptions: Compiler cleanup attributes have standard C23 semantics on every toolchain.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.

competencies.statement: Record which thread owns each lock and guarantee unlock on normal, error, cancellation, and early-return paths.
misconceptions: Freeing an object containing a locked mutex releases the lock safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record which thread owns each lock and guarantee unlock on normal, error, cancellation, and early-return paths.

competencies.statement: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.
misconceptions: A structure assignment safely duplicates ownership of every nested handle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design one lifecycle object that owns nested memory and operating-system resources with failure-atomic construction.

competencies.statement: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.
misconceptions: Aligned word reads and writes are automatically race-free.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.

competencies.statement: Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.
misconceptions: Atomic variables make neighboring non-atomic data visible in every order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.

competencies.statement: Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.
misconceptions: A release store alone prevents an object from being freed too soon.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.

competencies.statement: Compare locks, reference counts, hazard pointers, epochs, RCU-like schemes, and ownership transfer for safe reclamation.
misconceptions: Lock-free deletion may free a node immediately after unlinking it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare locks, reference counts, hazard pointers, epochs, RCU-like schemes, and ownership transfer for safe reclamation.

competencies.statement: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.
misconceptions: A clean ThreadSanitizer run proves all schedules race-free.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.

competencies.statement: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
misconceptions: A shared C header guarantees binary compatibility across all targets.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.

competencies.statement: Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.
misconceptions: Foreign callers may free an opaque C handle with their native allocator.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.

competencies.statement: Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.
misconceptions: A pointer and null terminator fully describe every foreign buffer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.

competencies.statement: Convert errors to stable status and out-value contracts and prevent language exceptions or panics from crossing unsupported C frames.
misconceptions: Rust panic or Python exception propagation through C is automatically ABI-safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert errors to stable status and out-value contracts and prevent language exceptions or panics from crossing unsupported C frames.

competencies.statement: Treat WebAssembly pointers as offsets into bounded linear memory with copy, growth, lifetime, and host-reference rules.
misconceptions: A WebAssembly pointer is a host pointer and remains valid after memory growth.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat WebAssembly pointers as offsets into bounded linear memory with copy, growth, lifetime, and host-reference rules.

competencies.statement: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.
misconceptions: ASan detects uninitialized reads, every leak, and every temporal defect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.

competencies.statement: Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fully instrumented MemorySanitizer environments for their distinct defect classes.
misconceptions: UBSan and MSan are interchangeable memory-error modes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fully instrumented MemorySanitizer environments for their distinct defect classes.

competencies.statement: Run Clang and GCC static analyzers, preserve paths and assumptions, classify findings, and regression-test repairs.
misconceptions: Static analyzer warnings are either proofs or ignorable false positives.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run Clang and GCC static analyzers, preserve paths and assumptions, classify findings, and regression-test repairs.

competencies.statement: Build a deterministic libFuzzer target with bounded input, sanitizer instrumentation, corpus, dictionary, timeout, and minimized regressions.
misconceptions: High line coverage means a parser has been fuzzed thoroughly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build a deterministic libFuzzer target with bounded input, sanitizer instrumentation, corpus, dictionary, timeout, and minimized regressions.

competencies.statement: Maintain a defect-by-tool and platform matrix so unsupported configurations and blind spots remain explicit.
misconceptions: Running every tool once is better than selecting compatible complementary gates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a defect-by-tool and platform matrix so unsupported configurations and blind spots remain explicit.

competencies.statement: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
misconceptions: The number of malloc calls alone explains memory performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.

competencies.statement: Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.
misconceptions: Smaller source code or fewer pointers guarantees better cache locality.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.

competencies.statement: Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.
misconceptions: A benchmark winner on one micro-test is the best allocator for production.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.

competencies.statement: Evaluate allocator checks, guard pages, canaries, randomization, control-flow defenses, fortification, and production cost.
misconceptions: Hardening flags repair undefined behavior in source code.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate allocator checks, guard pages, canaries, randomization, control-flow defenses, fortification, and production cost.

competencies.statement: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.
misconceptions: A faster benchmark permits weaker cleanup or failure behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.

competencies.statement: Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.
misconceptions: A large unit-test count substitutes for boundary and fault evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.

competencies.statement: Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.
misconceptions: Passing one debug compiler build proves optimized portability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.

competencies.statement: Pin source, dependencies, compiler, flags, generated files, symbols, SBOM, provenance, and artifact identity.
misconceptions: The same version label proves two native binaries are equivalent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin source, dependencies, compiler, flags, generated files, symbols, SBOM, provenance, and artifact identity.

competencies.statement: Canary immutable artifacts against memory, crash, latency, allocator, and correctness signals with a tested rollback trigger.
misconceptions: A rollback is safe without state and ABI compatibility evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Canary immutable artifacts against memory, crash, latency, allocator, and correctness signals with a tested rollback trigger.

competencies.statement: Preserve first-failure artifacts, symbolize safely, contain exposure, reproduce, repair causally, and verify recovery and prevention.
misconceptions: Restarting the process and adding memory is sufficient incident resolution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve first-failure artifacts, symbolize safely, contain exposure, reproduce, repair causally, and verify recovery and prevention.

credential.title: Memory Management and Memory Safety in Modern C23 Certificate
